data.summary: Stack-based rpnEval over + - * / with fail-closed throws + 6 assertions passing; committed.

Implemented capacity-bounded LRU cache on native Map insertion order (get+set both refresh recency, in-place set doesn't grow size) with a vitest suite covering plain eviction, get-refresh eviction, size bound, in-place update, and get-miss.

data.details:
1 commit(s):
calibration/rpnEval.test.ts | 16 ++++++++++++++++
 calibration/rpnEval.ts      | 30 ++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++
 2 files changed, 46 insertions(+)

✓ calibration/rpnEval.ts exports rpnEval evaluating +,-,*,/ via a stack over ints/decimals; throws on unknown token, operand underflow, and leftover operands. calibration/rpnEval.test.ts covers four valid expressions and two throwing (underflow + leftover) cases.: rpnEval.ts exports rpnEval using a number[] stack with all four operators in OPS; numeric regex ^[+-]?\d+(\.\d+)?$ accepts ints and decimals; explicit throws for unknown token, stack.length<2 underflow, and stack.length!==1 leftover. Test file has exactly four valid-expression assertions (3 4 +, 3 4 + 5 *, 10 2 /, 2 3 4 * +) and two throwing assertions: '3 +' (underflow) and '1 2 3 +' (leftover).

data.summary: parseCsvLine RFC-4180 parser + 5 vitest cases implemented, typecheck clean, all tests green; touched only the two calibration files.
Implemented semverCompare with full §11 precedence and a vitest test covering all required cases; all green.
parseDuration + tests implemented, verified via tsx, committed
Implemented deterministic lexical-tie-break topoSort (Kahn's, throws on cycle) in calibration/topoSort.ts with linear/diamond/cycle vitest tests, all verified green.

data.details:
1 commit(s):
calibration/lruCache.test.ts | 56 ++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++
 calibration/lruCache.ts      | 38 ++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++
 2 files changed, 94 insertions(+)

✓ calibration/lruCache.ts exports lruCache with get/set/size, capacity-bounded, recency refreshed by BOTH get and set, in-place update doesn't grow size: lruCache.ts exports lruCache returning {get,set,size}. Eviction loop in evict() enforces map.size <= capacity after every set. get() promotes via delete+reinsert on hit (recency refresh); set() deletes an existing key before reinsert (recency refresh without size growth, confirmed by the size()===1 assertion after double-set of 'a'). Capacity bound proven by the 100-sets test asserting size()===3.
✓ calibration/lruCache.test.ts covers plain eviction, get-refreshes-recency eviction, and the size bound: Test 1 covers plain eviction (a,b,c at capacity 2 evicts a). Test 2 covers get-refreshes-recency (get('a') before set('c') causes b to be evicted instead of a). Test 3 covers the size bound (100 sets into capacity 3, size()===3). Additional tests cover in-place update and miss-does-not-refresh.

1 commit(s):
calibration/parseCsvLine.test.ts | 20 ++++++++++++++++++++
 calibration/parseCsvLine.ts      | 32 ++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++
 2 files changed, 52 insertions(+)

✓ calibration/parseCsvLine.ts exports parseCsvLine handling quoted fields, escaped "" quotes, embedded commas inside quotes, empty fields, and empty input. calibration/parseCsvLine.test.ts covers plain, embedded-comma, escaped-quote, empty-middle-field, and empty-input cases.: parseCsvLine.ts exports parseCsvLine with a state-machine parser: inQuotes tracks quoted fields, the lookahead line[i+1]==='"' handles escaped "" as a literal quote, commas inside quotes accumulate into the field, consecutive commas yield empty fields, and empty input falls through the loop to the final fields.push(field) returning [""]. The test file contains exactly the five required cases — plain (a,b,c), embedded comma ("a,b",c), escaped quote ("she said ""hi"""), empty middle field (a,,c), and empty input ([""]) — each with correct expected outputs per RFC 4180.

1 commit(s):
calibration/semverCompare.test.ts | 29 +++++++++++++++++++++
 calibration/semverCompare.ts      | 54 +++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++
 2 files changed, 83 insertions(+)

✓ calibration/semverCompare.ts exports semverCompare returning -1|0|1 with correct numeric core + prerelease precedence (prerelease < release, numeric<alphanumeric, longer-outranks-shorter): semverCompare is exported with return type -1|0|1. Core major.minor.patch is compared numerically via cmpNum over parsed Number values (lexical trap avoided). Prerelease handling matches spec §11: preA===null/preB===null branches make release > prerelease; cmpPre ranks numeric identifiers below alphanumeric (an !== bn branch returns an ? -1 : 1), compares numeric pairs by value and alphanumeric pairs lexically, and falls through to cmpNum(a.length, b.length) so a longer identifier set outranks a shorter one when all preceding identifiers are equal. Logic is verifiably correct from the diff alone.
✓ calibration/semverCompare.test.ts covers core ordering, equality, prerelease-vs-release, and two prerelease-identifier cases: Test file contains: core ordering (including the 1.10.0 vs 1.9.0 numeric-not-lexical case), equality (two exact-equal assertions returning 0), prerelease-vs-release in both directions (1.0.0-alpha < 1.0.0 and inverse), and two distinct prerelease-identifier cases: numeric<alphanumeric (alpha.1 < alpha.beta) and longer-outranks-shorter (alpha.1 > alpha). All assertions are consistent with the implementation's semantics.

1 commit(s):
calibration/parseDuration.test.ts | 29 +++++++++++++++++++++++++++++
 calibration/parseDuration.ts      | 32 ++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++
 2 files changed, 61 insertions(+)

✓ calibration/parseDuration.ts exports parseDuration returning ms; handles ms/s/m/h/d, multi-unit, case-insensitive, whitespace; throws on empty/unknown-unit/negative: File exports parseDuration returning a number in ms. UNITS covers ms/s/m/h/d; TOKEN regex with /gi flag plus toLowerCase handles case-insensitivity; multi-unit works via matchAll accumulation ('1h30m', '2d 4h'); whitespace tolerated via \s* in the token and space-stripped residual comparison. Throws on empty (trim check), negative (/-\d/ check), and unknown unit/missing number (fail-closed residual check catches '5x' and partial parses like '10ms5x'). 'ms' alternation ordered before 'm' so '500ms' binds correctly.
✓ calibration/parseDuration.test.ts covers 90s, 1h30m, 500ms, 2d, case-insensitivity, and two throwing cases: Tests assert 90s→90000, 1h30m→5400000, 500ms→500, 2d→172800000, case-insensitive '1H30M'→5400000, plus a whitespace multi-unit case; exactly two throwing cases present (empty input, unknown unit '5x'). All expected values are arithmetically correct.

1 commit(s):
calibration/topoSort.test.ts | 20 ++++++++++++++++++++
 calibration/topoSort.ts      | 41 +++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++
 2 files changed, 61 insertions(+)

✓ calibration/topoSort.ts exports topoSort returning a valid deterministic (lexical tie-break) ordering of all nodes; throws with "cycle" in the message on a cyclic graph: topoSort is exported and implements Kahn's algorithm with ready.sort() before each pop, giving a deterministic ascending-lexical tie-break among ready nodes; all nodes from edges are registered via ensure() and emitted; on a cycle, out.length !== indeg.size triggers throw new Error containing the word 'cycle'.
✓ calibration/topoSort.test.ts covers a linear chain, a diamond (a first / d last / predecessors before d), and a cycle throwing: Test file contains exactly these three cases: linear chain a->b->c asserting full ordering; diamond asserting a is first, d is last, and both b and c precede d; and a two-node cycle asserting topoSort throws matching /cycle/.
